item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that any article that uses temporal/dimensional travel stuff and reverse-anti-nullification fields should either be short and punchy, or be worth the time investment. This wasn't really either.

Also, I might be crazy, but I thought I saw a few non-union Mexican extradimensional equivalents of classic articles. This was another element that was more distracting than engaging.

item.title: Re: SCP-4023
item.content:encoded: And just like that I have become the first, final, and ONLY meme-ber of the O25 council.

item.title: Re: Meh
item.content:encoded: Honestly I totally agree, my suggestion would be the first main anomaly is the 23 meme and there are a few addenda that get progressively less and less SCP worthy and it turns out your researcher Ike is just infected with the 23 meme trying to make explanations for the number haunting him. That would be an actual interesting read, but as it stands, meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly due to time constraints I had to stop about halfway through, and I dont intend to finish it, it started off with some breaks in professional tone then said unprofessional tone just got worse as it went on. The note from the O5 portion was what sealed this article's fate for me, honestly, because 1) it makes no sense to me to add an O6 council for the purpose of this one article, and 2) it paints the O5 council as a bunch of loose-lips-sink-ships buffoons. If you tightened up the dialogue and the tone, it could be interesting enough for me to finish reading, but as it stands I can't.

-1

item.title: Interesting potential, but disappointing payoff.
item.content:encoded:
You had biblical serpents, anomalous dice, and a whole bunch of other interesting anomalies and I was expecting to see them either interact somehow, or come together broken god style into some sort of mega anomaly, but no, instead you've got "mysterious extra dimensional creature wants to take over the o5s so here's o6, something that serves no purpose except to get to a number we want to get to so we can have 23 ghost!" and that's just such a disappointing ending

I'm not sure if you can rewrite already posted scps, but if you can
-make the 23 anomalies (and I guess the placeholder redacted/expunged, which you can tell are just fillers because you ran out of ideas) work together some how or are related in some way other than just numbers, rather than the lost and found box of scps with 23s

-Maybe make your named dr more of a character than a plot delivery device? You could really do something with the blue dialogue boxes, but you don't right now.

-Scrap the o6 council, you don't need it, just make a new ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Idk if this was the authors intent, but when I read it I interpreted the reveal at the end as scp 4023 had already infiltrated the world because while the 05 ans 06 councils have only 22 members together the administrator makes that 23 and therefore the requirements wouldve been met

item.title: Eh.
item.content:encoded:
Apollyon, other-dimensional being trying to invade and take over the 05s, aaaand how do they solve it? Wait, thats it?

+ Spoilers
- You better have read the article...

Do they just expand the number of bureaucrats???? And this just halts an Apollyon? Aren't they supposed to be inevitable apocalypses?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, since MTF-Alpha 1 wasn't a mobile task force then so the O6-Council had no task force to defend them. No wonder all O6-Council members died.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do not mean to be rude but I legitimately don't understand why this has so many upvotes.

The core premise is decent enough, but the entire thing is very meandering and rather derivative. More damningly, there are a lot of grammar and even a few spelling mistakes that just make the thing difficult to read on a very basic level. I'd like to see this improved someday but as it is now it's a definite -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read it all the way through, but I can't offer my normal +1 for your time.
Its obv you are going to 23 addenda, that there's some grand conspiracy so there's no surprise regarding anything. I can't tell who is talking to whom, so you're either relying on people knowing a lot of backstory or some format screw.

If I peeled this potato and took it to the core, it's what… a researcher discovering an anomaly that must be retroactively contained. That's fair. But your narrative just doesn't deliver and I can't suspend belief for 'O6 council'.
-1

item.title: Cool article
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed reading, but the dialogue between characters was kind of hard to follow since there was no label on who was talking. Grammar errors everywhere, but it was cool. I do agree series v is tryhard. I think scps should be a mix of series 1 types and series 4 types, not just all of them being crazy elaborate labyrinths. There should also be more scps like 2212.

item.title: Meh
item.content:encoded:
This would have been more interesting if it was just based on the memetic one without this whole silly Apollyon narrative and not to mention the fact that you are basically trying to make this a really badly done origin story.

I saw this scp several months ago and thought it was still bad and riddled with grammatical errors but it had potential so I didn’t vote, and especially since I assumed it would be deleted by now. So you can guess my surprise to see that it went pretty positive since I’ve been gone for whatever reason.

The obsessed with 23 meme is literally the only interesting factor here. A reason that Dr. Ike is so obsessed with this anomaly and a reason it would eventually drive him mad is all it needs to be, but you take it way too far and on the process copy so many tropes. You copy 05’s tale, you copy the hostile entity trying to invade our reality or whatever, the only original thing here is the obsession with 23 and you still blew that way out of proportion by trying to tie it in with the whole origin of the foundation and it just honestly does not work at all, especially when we have sooo many other amazing origin stories that work great and tie into a lot of things and aren’t completely silly (see the 001s among other things). Not that everything has to fit perfectly into this neat little canon or whatever because it absolutely doesn’t but this just turns its cheek to the entire history of the SCP Foundation that we already had and for what? This silly little 23 narrative? The big bad scary hostile entity narrative? I just don’t get it

In my honest opinion, this needs some large revisions and edits to ever be considered a decent scp. I hate to be so harsh but I’m only so harsh anyways because, like I said, I saw some serious potential here and am amazed/appalled that people are accepting this so readily for the waste of a slot that it is in its current state.

Definitely a -1

Edit: Just want to say I am really amazed that this post is still unchanged over a year later despite the fact that the author is still active on the site and could easily take any of the like 2 whole pages of critical comments into consideration. Even just fixing some of the spelling mistakes would be cool but nope, might as well leave a post be if it reaches the minimum to not be deleted I guess

There are literally grammatical errors in the VERY FIRST sentence in the article. Yet it’s still +79 and has been unchanged for over a year. Like I said, absolutely amazing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm pretty on the fence about this one. You have all the ingredients for a delicious Apollyon souffle, but by the time you're done cooking the reader is somehow left with lukewarm numberical anomaly soup. As has been commented on already, not enough payoff for the length.

You were clearly inspired while writing this, and I do enjoy reading about weird numbers. However, the tone of the O5 is noticeably imitative, and I feel you would be better off going in a non-O5's Tale direction for that addendum; there are additional wider issues with tone throughout the piece that make for an awkward read.

I would feel comfortable upvoting, except for this: hella typos. One or two is pretty normal, especially in articles of this size, but you're hitting double-digits here. I'd be happy to clean them up if you'd rather not re-analyze a piece you spent plenty of time looking at already. I ultimately like this article a lot, with the caveat that it needs a thorough tone and pacing revision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's been up a long time and there are still so many grammatical errors in here. Multiple "than"s that should be "then"s, and one "overcomed" which is the point at which I stopped.
The fact that I'm having to correct this just stopped it from being reasonable or realistic. There were all manner of conjugation errors. I'm not an educated person, but I have my doubts that a student would actually get their degree with this level of errors in their work, let alone be scouted by a powerful scientific organization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An enjoyable enough read. Good concept, fun execution, and the ending is pretty solid. Leaves it up in the air, which is something people haven't seemed to notice somehow? Theres two ways I see for this to be taken. First is the given: There is an actual eldritch thing obsessing over the number 23 that is plotting an elaborate calendar-man worthy takeover of the world. Or theres the "red herring is true" twist, where the earlier answer, of the SCP being a memetic hazard that drives people to obsess over the number 23 is true and the outcome is actually the O5's and everyone else falling into its trap.

The multiple-choice ending is nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I thought the omission of the "the"s and "a"s throughout the article were on purpose, and I even started counting them, hoping to see 23 missing determiners. I take it that, if this skip had me counting grammatical errors, then it's certainly a good piece of writing. Happily +1'd.

item.title: Potential Error
item.content:encoded:
"…all on site personnel has been reinformed…"

Did you mean to say "… all on site personnel have been reinformed…"

EDIT: "This obsession is goes through multiple stages described below."

Did you mean to say "This obsession goes…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate to add to the broken record here, but could you please go through this with a fine toothed comb and fix some grammatical errors? Otherwise and overall I did enjoy this SCP, that just made it hard to be immersed in the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP kinda feels like the first arc from the Antimemetics Division Hub. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The entry for the key has a typo near the bottom ("here" instead of "where").

In multiple areas of the containment procedures for the different objects, you have written "has/have to be" instead of "must be". This is just a nitpick as I have never read an SCP that has "has to be" or "have to be" in their containment procedures. Just seems too unprofessional for the foundation imo.

Other than that nice work, but was a bit too long for me and the reveal was slightly underwhelming. Could make the Appolyon entity sound a little scarier maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, and let's avoid giving this exactly 23 upvotes ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but sure, listing 23 scips is a hassle but the point of is clear in the end. So if I'd have any criticism is that it get a bit long and the dramatic curve is flatlining alot.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This post is interesting to read through and enjoy while having many grammatical mistakes scattered throughout it. It is the true neutral.

+1 though, I thought it was pretty cool.

item.title: Re: SCP-4023
item.content:encoded: This, folks, is the ultimate answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a really fun read :)

Entertained me until fortnite finished downloading.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand what this is trying to do here, but a lot got lost in the execution. I'm almost inclined to believe that this was a coldpost for a few reasons.

+ Show reasons
- Hide reasons
  • It seems like you bit off more than you could chew, putting 23 anomalies into one article and redacting half of them. Really, within the entire article, only like 4 or 5 of the SCP-4023-# entries are really even anomalous by their own right (and even fewer could actually stand as SCPs on their own), a good chunk of them are filler [DATA EXPUNGED] entries to take up space (with a handwavey in-universe explanation to boot), and the rest of them could be listed under addenda while still retaining the meaning of the article.
  • It is too damn long while trying too hard, making it too ambitious for its own good. It ends up failing to deliver on the promises it makes and builds up, leaving the reader feeling like they wasted their time reading a story with an unsatisfying/incomplete conclusion.
  • SO. MUCH. REDACTION. Seriously, it's all over the place, and it's in places where it really isn't needed, or is even given to the reader anyways, either upon a highlight or just through reading a later collapsible.
  • Despite failing to come up with 23 related SCPs (which appears to be the intention of the article, as well as the focus of the main format screw), a few of the articles that were mostly fleshed out were fairly pointless from a storytelling perspective. A ritual where a level-3 researcher has to murder some personal effects of the O-5 Council is just so out of left field, and it's just left hanging there. Same goes for the album. Literally none of that besides the number 23 had anything to do with the rest of the article. It read like a sandbox idea that you weren't comfortable with shoving into its own spot on the mainlist.
  • Lastly (and perhaps most importantly), there are a LOT of grammatical errors all over this thing. I get having a few mistakes here and there, but there are so many that it almost makes me think you pulled a Red Bull fueled all-nighter to crank this thing out. A line-by-line critique would help a lot with this.
- Hide reasons

TL;DR: Basically, this reads like something I would find in the draft forums, not like a finished work. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean. It has potential, but needs heavy tweaking. A bit of slimming down here and there wouldn't go amiss either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Stylistic choice on the part of the author here. Seems like they're letting the reader have the choice to read some parts as redacted or not in this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this is just trying way, way too hard to be meta. I lost interest as it dragged on. It's just a bunch of unrelated skips, several of which are pretty insubstantial. And the bit about the ritual involving overseer items is just… No.
I get what you're doing, but it's just not a great idea, and there's already a movie about the number 23.
Also, there is too much [redacted] that doesn't really serve any purpose and instead just feels like lazy writing.
I say salvage a few of the better items for their own sakes and trash this entry.

I'm not saying this to be mean, I just don't think this entry as a whole works very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What? I'm sorry but that makes no sense. Redactions are…redactions, dude. It's redacted, as in, redacted. Like removed. Redacted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me, or does it sort of feel like a lot of the Series 5 entries so far are kinda trying too hard?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Actually meant" by whom?

Like… are we talking in-universe, or by the author's intent? Because neither one really makes much sense to me.

If it's in-universe, just… why? And if it's purely for the audience's benefit, why not just make up some numbers for the dates?

item.title: Re: SCP-4023
item.content:encoded: That's easy. 24.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Show obvious visual gag
- Hide overused meme

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like this article would be substantially better if it were pared down - less ambitious, less audacious. Not every article has to introduce a major new thing to the universe, or be a Grand Unified Theory behind a whole bunch of SCPs. IMO it would be better just being a small interesting thing that haunted a group of researchers for a while. No need to create an O6 council (that annoyed me), no need to have Mary Nakayama, no need for the clear comparison between 4023 and the Factory. Plus some of the symptoms caused by the anomalous objects were… just too much.

Just too much melodrama. It needs to be smaller and more self-contained, and not lean on every other part of the universe so much while attempting to change them and multiplying entities.

(Like other readers said, there are also quite a lot of obvious grammar errors.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this was some damn good build-up I don't feel the reveal is worth it and was somewhat underwhelming.

item.title: Re: SCP-4023
item.content:encoded: Could you please explain continuity errors? I think I am already seeing the grammar problems but I am not sure what exactly did you mean by continuity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ████ redactions are readable on purpose. They are meant to expand upon something if you want it to but they can also be hiden if you like things redacted. The "properly" redacted stuff, is actually meant to be redacted.

item.title: Re: SCP-4023
item.content:encoded:
You definitely could stand to go back through with an editor and tighten up dialogue and fix some continuity and grammar errors.

I was initially hooked, but gradually discovered it was very old hat as I went and so lost interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot of grammatical errors. Also your bl█ck bar redactions can be read through when highlighted, not sure if this was intentional since you did use pr█per redaction on one of the dates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Normaly I would try to come up with an excuse as to why that is and what i was thinking at the time.

Not this time. I honestly have no idea how that went past me. If there are more mistakes like this one than I get the downvotes.

Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Dice" is plural but the first collapsible only has the one die. I almost fixed this myself, but I wasn't sure if you'd want to change the title text too.

item.title: SCP-4023
item.content:encoded:
How many times is too many when it comes to number 23?

So I may have rushed the posting because I wanted a specific number for this, hope it doesn't bite me in the ass too hard. I am open to any post-posting criticism. Hope you enjoy the skip.

Oh and big shout-out to CyrusFiredawn, who helped me with the draft.
